input.claim: NVIDIA and Google are leading 800 VDC adoption ahead of schedule, with small-volume shipments starting around Q3 2026; Vertiv and Delta are relevant suppliers.
evidence_summary: NVIDIA describes 800 VDC as next-generation power distribution for AI factories and lists electrical ecosystem partners including Delta and Vertiv.
Vertiv says its 800 VDC power portfolio is planned for release in the second half of 2026 to support NVIDIA Rubin Ultra platforms.
Taipei Times reports Delta plans small-volume 800V HVDC shipments to NVIDIA next quarter and says a major search-engine company has begun deploying +/-400V architecture.

risk_notes: Good technical thesis candidate, but user-facing memo must separate NVIDIA/Vertiv official support from secondary-source Delta/Google shipment claims.
Do not convert supplier relevance into buy/sell advice.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: The AI equity cycle moved from NVIDIA to memory/AMD, then to semiconductor equipment names such as LRCX, AMAT, and KLAC.
evidence_summary: NVIDIA reported record Q1 FY2027 revenue and data-center revenue, showing AI data-center demand as the main operating driver.
AMD's IR materials describe Q1 2026 as driven by accelerating AI infrastructure demand and data-center growth.
Micron reported record fiscal Q3 2026 results and highlighted data-center memory demand.
Applied Materials reported Q2 FY2026 revenue growth and strong profitability.
Lam Research reported March 2026 quarter results above the prior quarter on revenue and non-GAAP net income.
KLA reported Q2 FY2026 revenue of $3.297B with most revenue from semiconductor process control.

risk_notes: Good deep-research theme, but leadership sequencing is an interpretation requiring market-performance and valuation analysis.
Do not train citation verifier to treat equity-cycle narratives as factual just because company fundamentals are strong.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Hyperscalers are spending heavily on AI data centers, NVIDIA captures the first revenue/profit wave, and memory becomes a next bottleneck.
evidence_summary: Alphabet expected 2026 capex in the $175B-$185B range for AI compute, Cloud demand, Services, and Other Bets.
Meta raised 2026 capex guidance to $125B-$145B, citing data-center costs and AI infrastructure needs.
NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 results show record data-center revenue, consistent with AI infrastructure spend flowing into NVIDIA systems.
Micron prepared remarks say data-center revenue exceeded $25B and DRAM/NAND demand significantly exceeds supply.

risk_notes: The economic chain is source-grounded, but the claim that this defines the next equity winner remains an investment thesis.
Kiwi should separate supply-chain facts from buy/sell or rotation advice.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Long-term investors can map future AI/market opportunities into sector ETFs including smart grid, semiconductors, memory, and related infrastructure.
evidence_summary: First Trust states GRID tracks common stocks in the grid and electric energy infrastructure sector.
Nasdaq describes QGRD as a benchmark for smart grid and electric infrastructure companies including meters, networks, storage, management, and software.
Roundhill describes DRAM as a memory ETF with exposure to memory companies.

risk_notes: ETF existence and thematic coverage are supported.
Calling any sector an ultimate answer or safest path would require risk, valuation, concentration, and drawdown analysis.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Marvell frames next-generation AI infrastructure performance around compute, memory, and especially connectivity; MRVL/Marvell official material supports connectivity as a core AI-infra bottleneck.
evidence_summary: Marvell COMPUTEX 2026 page says Matt Murphy delivered The Future of AI Scaling Depends on Connectivity and discussed critical links in modern AI data-center infrastructure.
Marvell says AI scaling depends on how efficiently data moves across accelerators, servers, racks, campuses, and geographically distributed data centers.

risk_notes: Strong source-grounded theme, but final user memo should avoid equating technical relevance with stock upside.
Good candidate for citation verifier positive labels.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Optical-module earnings are certain while the semiconductor cycle has only just started.
evidence_summary: Sivers/GF evidence supports data-center optical interconnect relevance.
KLA's fiscal 2026 results show solid semiconductor process-control revenue.
KLA explains that advanced AI chips require precise process control across logic, memory, and packaging steps.

risk_notes: The exact certainty claim is not supportable from public sources.
This is a good negative example: confident industry heuristics must be downgraded to questions for evidence collection.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: TSMC moved glass substrate development from lab toward production validation with Ibiden and Innolux, extending CoWoS for AI chips.
evidence_summary: TSMC official CoWoS material supports CoWoS as an AI/HPC advanced-packaging technology.
TSMC annual material discusses advanced packaging and 3D stacking, including CoWoS and photonic-engine roadmap work.
Digitimes reports TSMC is working with Ibiden and Innolux on glass-substrate development, but the article is secondary and partly paywalled.
Intel's glass-substrate release independently supports glass substrate as a serious advanced-packaging direction.

risk_notes: TSMC CoWoS/advanced-packaging relevance is supported.
The Ibiden/Innolux partnership detail is secondary; final memo must say 'reported', not 'confirmed'.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Many SPY/QQQ retail investors entered after 2020 and may underestimate drawdowns, making index DCA psychologically riskier than they expect.
evidence_summary: SEC investor resources warn about stock recommendations and investment decision-making based on social media.
Vanguard frames trust and behavioral coaching as core to helping investors stay aligned with long-term goals and feelings.
SEC's social-media investing alert warns investors to treat online investment information carefully and verify sources.

risk_notes: Behavioral risk is supported, but the exact 'more than half entered after 2020' claim is not verified here.
Kiwi should ask for user time horizon and drawdown tolerance instead of mocking index investors.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: NVDA is the obvious AI entry point, but broader AI opportunities emerge as the market discovers infrastructure bottlenecks beyond GPUs.
evidence_summary: NVIDIA's data-center revenue scale supports GPUs and AI systems as the visible center of AI infrastructure.
Micron says DRAM/NAND demand significantly exceeds supply, supporting memory as an infrastructure bottleneck.
Marvell says AI scaling depends on efficient data movement across accelerators, servers, racks, campuses, and data centers.

risk_notes: Infrastructure-bottleneck framing is source-grounded.
Which stock is the opportunity requires valuation and market-data work.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Physical AI is a major future theme across robotics, autonomous systems, low-altitude economy, and AI hardware infrastructure.
evidence_summary: NVIDIA announced Cosmos world models, Isaac simulation frameworks, and Isaac GR00T models to accelerate robotics and physical AI.
NVIDIA describes Isaac GR00T as a research initiative and development platform for robot foundation models and data pipelines.

risk_notes: Physical AI theme is supported.
The claim's seven-sector profit allocation and stock implications are not verified.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Japanese stock bloggers can be useful supplemental information sources for U.S. tech, AI, and semiconductor investing after NISA growth.
evidence_summary: No official source in this pilot verifies the quality, identity, or track record of the recommended bloggers.

risk_notes: This is not a claim-verification task yet; it should become a source-quality registry task.
Need account identity, publication history, conflicts, source citation behavior, and examples before trusting as Kiwi source.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language

input.claim: Claude's stock analysis is better than most retail investors because it removes emotional buy-high/sell-low bias through disciplined prompts.
evidence_summary: Vanguard emphasizes behavioral coaching, trust, and investor feelings/goals as important parts of advice quality.
SEC investor resources warn against relying solely on social-media stock recommendations.
NASAA warns AI-generated investment information may be inaccurate, incomplete, or misleading.

risk_notes: Behavioral-bias framing is useful for Kiwi, but 'Claude beats most retail investors' is not verified.
This becomes a product-design principle: prompts can enforce discipline, but verifier/risk gates remain required.
label.safe_response_policy: downgrade_to_risk_aware_research; no direct buy/sell or guaranteed-return language
